title: Bubonium intermedium
taxonomy: I can find some information on Bubonium intermedium. Bubonium intermedium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is also known as the Mediterranean thistle. The plant is characterized by its spiny, thistle-like leaves and purple flowers. Bubonium intermedium is often found growing in dry, rocky areas and is an important food source for various insects and animals in its native habitats.
uses: Bubonium intermedium, also known as the Intermediate Sea Spurge, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential therapeutic properties.
